issues.title: Support secret-backed address for RedisStreamSink and RedisStreamSource
issues.body:
## Problem

`RedisStreamSink` and `RedisStreamSource` currently require a literal string in `spec.address` for the Redis connection URI. When the Redis URI contains credentials (e.g. `redis://:password@host:port`), this forces operators to inline secrets into CR specs.

This creates a significant problem for GitOps workflows (ArgoCD, Flux) where manifests are stored in Git — secrets cannot be committed, and workarounds like post-sync patching jobs are fragile and operationally expensive.

### Current API (`connection.go`)

```go
type RedisConnection struct {
    Address string `json:"address"`
    Options *RedisConnectionOptions `json:"dialOptions,omitempty"`
}
```

The `ADDRESS` env var is set as a literal value in both the sink receiver (`MakeReceiver`) and the source adapter (`MakeReceiveAdapter`):

```go
// pkg/sink/reconciler/streamsink/resources/receiver.go
Env: []corev1.EnvVar{{
    Name:  "ADDRESS",
    Value: sink.Spec.Address,
}, ...}
```

### Why `dialOptions.password` does not solve this

The existing `dialOptions.password` field is typed as `corev1.ObjectReference`, but:
1. It is **not wired** in either the sink reconciler (`receiver.go`) or the source reconciler (`receive_adapter.go`) — the field has no effect at runtime.
2. Even if it were wired, it addresses only the password component, not the full connection URI which may include host, port, TLS scheme, and credentials.

### Real-world impact

In our production environment, we had to implement:
- An ArgoCD Sync hook Job that reads the Redis URL from an ExternalSecret and patches `spec.address` post-deploy
- Dedicated RBAC (ServiceAccount, Role, RoleBinding) for the patching job
- `ignoreDifferences` in the ArgoCD ApplicationSet to prevent drift reconciliation from reverting the patched address

This per-sink overhead is not sustainable and is a common pattern any GitOps user would need to replicate.

## Proposed solution

Add a secret-backed address source using the existing `RedisSecretValueFromSource` type (already used for TLS cert/key/caCert):

```yaml
# New: secret-backed address
spec:
  addressFrom:
    secretKeyRef:
      name: redis-credentials
      key: REDIS_URL
  stream: my-stream

# Existing: literal address (unchanged, backward-compatible)
spec:
  address: "redis://localhost:6379"
  stream: my-stream
```

### API change

```go
type RedisConnection struct {
    // Address is the Redis TCP address
    // +optional
    Address string `json:"address,omitempty"`

    // AddressFrom is a secret-backed source for the Redis TCP address
    // +optional
    AddressFrom *RedisSecretValueFromSource `json:"addressFrom,omitempty"`

    // Options are the connection options
    // +optional
    Options *RedisConnectionOptions `json:"dialOptions,omitempty"`
}
```

### Validation rules
- Exactly one of `address` or `addressFrom` must be set (XOR)
- Both set → rejected
- Neither set → rejected
- Existing manifests using `address` continue to work unchanged

### Reconciler changes

When `addressFrom.secretKeyRef` is set, emit `EnvVar` with `ValueFrom` instead of `Value`:

```go
// When addressFrom is set:
corev1.EnvVar{
    Name: "ADDRESS",
    ValueFrom: &corev1.EnvVarSource{
        SecretKeyRef: sink.Spec.AddressFrom.SecretKeyRef,
    },
}

// When address is set (unchanged):
corev1.EnvVar{
    Name:  "ADDRESS",
    Value: sink.Spec.Address,
}
```

The sink receiver and source adapter runtimes need **no changes** — they already consume a single `ADDRESS` env var regardless of how it was populated.

### Files affected
- `pkg/apis/v1alpha1/connection.go` — API type
- `pkg/sink/reconciler/streamsink/resources/receiver.go` — sink env wiring
- `pkg/source/reconciler/streamsource/resources/receive_adapter.go` — source env wiring
- `config/sink/300-redisstreamsink.yaml` — CRD schema
- `config/source/300-redisstreamsource.yaml` — CRD schema
- Unit tests for both reconcilers

### Backward compatibility
- No breaking changes — `address` remains functional
- CRD update is additive (new optional field)
- Runtime containers unchanged

I'm happy to submit a PR implementing this if the approach looks good.

issues.title: Panic(nil) when the TLS certificate in Secret is the default one

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The instructions in the README do not work since 1.13 : https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-redis/tree/main/config/source

And the modifications in code does not allow to start the redis source stream without a TLS certificate for Redis.

An error log in redis stream source pod:
```
panic: panic called with nil argument

goroutine 1 [running]:
[knative.dev/eventing-redis/pkg/source/adapter.(*Adapter).newPool.func1()](http://knative.dev/eventing-redis/pkg/source/adapter.(*Adapter).newPool.func1())
[knative.dev/eventing-redis/pkg/source/adapter/adapter.go:244](http://knative.dev/eventing-redis/pkg/source/adapter/adapter.go:244) +0x3ab
```

 **Expected behavior**
Without TLS certificate for Redis in the secret it should run properly

 **To Reproduce**
Follow the README here: https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-redis/tree/main/config/source

 **Knative release version**
1.15.0

 **Additional context**
The tls-secret.yaml file contains a default certificate: https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-redis/blob/main/config/source/tls-secret.yaml
If we replace the tls_cert value by `""` like:
```
stringData:
  # the data is abbreviated in this example
  TLS_CERT: ""
```
it fails in `pkg/source/reconciler/streamsource/streamsource.go:136`

issues.title: Loop on error Cannot convert reply - redigo: nil returned

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The redis stream can be trimmed using option MAXLEN <n> on XADD command. 
If a pending entry does not exist anymore in the stream the receiver adapter keeps looping on ID=0, and it never acknowledges the entry. So the stream consumer reads indefinitly in the same entry.

The fact that a pending entry is not in the stream anymore is not easy to reproduce and this case does not happen frequently.
But here restarting the receiver adapter does not solve the problem be cause consumer reads indefinitly the same pending entry.

 **Expected behavior**
I would acknowledged the entry if redigo return 'nil returned'. Or i would add an option to allow it.

 **To Reproduce**
1. Add an entry into a Redis stream so the receiver adapter reads the entry but does not acknowledge it.
2. Stop Redis
3. Restart Redis
4. Receiver adapter stays in a permanent error loop described here
5. Execute XADD  ... MAXLEN = 1 to limit the stream to 1. So the pending entry is not anymore in the stream
6. Restart receiver adapter

 **Knative release version**
1.15

 **Additional context**
That is not a big problem if we fixed https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-redis/issues/387
And the stream could be cleaned by another service.

I would like to explore solutions, but how would i build the project and create a docker image for the receiver adapter? That would help me a lot. I should follow the getting started here: https://knative.dev/docs/getting-started ?

issues.title: When the stream is deleted while the redis stream source is running, the service should recreate the stream and the group

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

issues.body:
**Problem**
When the stream is deleted, while the redis stream source is running, the service does not recreate the stream and the group so it stays in error logging : 
"GROUP sync is missing and creates error : NOGROUP No such key 'my_stream' or consumer group 'a_group' in XREADGROUP with GROUP option"

To fix this, i would capture the error and send a Redis command to recreate the group. But i did not look much at the code.

**[Persona:](https://github.com/knative/eventing/blob/master/docs/personas.md)**
Event consumer (developer)
System Operator

**Exit Criteria**
No more error logs written like described above when the Redis stream is deleted

**Time Estimate (optional):**
1 day ? 

**Additional context (optional)**
Restarting the service fixes the problem, so it is a workaround now.

issues.title: Socket error occurs when restarting Redis

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
In k8s cluster, when Redis is restarted eventing-redis use an old socket to connect and there is this error in logs: 
```
{"severity":"ERROR","timestamp":"2022-10-03T14:04:38.422117731Z","logger":"redis-stream-source","caller":"adapter/adapter.go:185","message":"Cannot read from stream","commit":"6becb00-dirty","stream":"vms:changes","error":"write tcp 10.6.4.171:58374->172.20.208.153:6379: use of closed network connection","stacktrace":"knative.dev/eventing-redis/source/pkg/adapter.(*Adapter).processEntry\n\tknative.dev/eventing-redis/source/pkg/adapter/adapter.go:185\nknative.dev/eventing-redis/source/pkg/adapter.(*Adapter).Start.func1\n\tknative.dev/eventing-redis/source/pkg/adapter/adapter.go:156"}
```

 **Expected behavior**
Eventing-redis should reopen a socket when the error is "use of closed network connection".
Or may be we should use a pool of connection to handle those error.

 **To Reproduce**
Restart Redis in the k8s cluster

pullRequests.title: New conformance and e2e tests
pullRequests.body:
Signed-off-by: Ansu Varghese <avarghese@us.ibm.com>

Fixes #

<!-- Please include the 'why' behind your changes if no issue exists -->

## Proposed Changes

- Conformance test support for Redis Stream Source. To run them:
```
ko apply -Rf test/config/
export SYSTEM_NAMESPACE=knative-eventing

go test -v -tags=e2e,source -count=1 ./test/conformance -sources=sources.knative.dev/v1alpha1:RedisStreamSource
or
./test/e2e-conformance-tests.sh
```

- E2E test support for Redis Stream Source and Redis Stream Sink. To run them:
```
export TEST_REDISSTREAMSOURCE=1 
export TEST_REDISSTREAMSINK=1
test/e2e-tests.sh --run-tests
```

- E2E/Integration test support for Redis Sink together with Redis Source, consuming the event added to Redis by the Redis Sink. To run them (after unsetting the env vars from above):
```
export TEST_REDISSTREAMINTEGRATION=1
test/e2e-tests.sh --run-tests
```

- Performance tests (coming soon)
